title: OIE Community Store manifest (oie.json)
description: Per-release manifest read at each release tag (or, for collections with no releases, at the default-branch tip). Two shapes: a SINGLE manifest describing one extension, or a COLLECTION whose 'items' array lists many installable pieces of content (channels, code templates, code template libraries) or extensions in one repository.

type.description: Extension/content category. plugin, connector, and datatype are binary extensions installed through the engine's extension installer (restart required). channel, code-template, and code-template-library are content, imported through the engine's APIs with no restart.

storeDocs.description: Repo-relative path to markdown rendered in the store detail view. Must be relative (no leading '/', no '..').

ui.description: UI surfaces the package ships: 'web' (web administrator) and/or 'swing' (Swing Administrator). An empty array means server-only (no UI). Omit the field to be shown in both stores.

single.description: One extension per repository — the artifact is a .zip asset on the GitHub release, paired with a <asset>.zip.sha256 sidecar.

id.description: Stable extension id. MUST equal the 'path' attribute of the extension's plugin.xml — that is how installed-version tracking works.

version.description: MUST equal the release tag, modulo an optional leading 'v'.

filename.description: Which release asset to install when the release has more than one .zip. Supports a {version} placeholder.

collection.description: Many items in one repository (e.g. an examples repo). Each item's artifact is a repo-relative file fetched raw at the release tag — or at the default-branch tip when the repository has no releases, so content collections publish with just a push.

id.description: Stable id for this item, unique across the whole store.

artifact.description: Repo-relative path of the installable file (channel/code-template/library XML). Plain path — the store URL-encodes it.

contentId.description: REQUIRED in practice for content items: the engine id (UUID) inside the artifact, used for installed-detection.

checksumUrl.description: Optional absolute URL of a sha256 sidecar for the artifact.
